_description: Extreme anomaly use cases designed to trigger Autoencoder and Statistical models
_usage: Inject these records into test/validation data to verify detection across all models

use_cases.name: UC-01: Active Ransomware Attack
use_cases.description: Endpoint with active ransomware - multiple threats, infected, quarantined, encrypted apps disabled. Triggers Statistical via activeThreats z-score >>3. Triggers Autoencoder via simultaneous deviation across threat, network, and status features.

expected_triggers.autoencoder: Never-seen combination: infected=true + networkQuarantineEnabled=true + activeThreats>0 + disconnected + scanStatus=aborted

use_cases.name: UC-02: Crypto-Mining Hijack
use_cases.description: Server with abnormally high CPU/memory indicating crypto-mining. Normal totalMemory ~20K but this shows massive allocation. Triggers Statistical via totalMemory z-score. Triggers Autoencoder via resource+threat pattern mismatch.

expected_triggers.autoencoder: Extreme resource values + infected + hasContainerizedWorkload=true (never seen in training) + scanStatus=started (never seen)

use_cases.name: UC-03: Insider Threat - Decommissioned Agent Reactivated
use_cases.description: Previously decommissioned endpoint suddenly active with threats - suggests stolen credentials or rogue device. Multiple boolean flags flipped from training norms.

expected_triggers.statistical: activeThreats z-score=7.0; 6 boolean fields flipped from zero-variance training norms
expected_triggers.autoencoder: isDecommissioned+isPendingUninstall+infected+threatRebootRequired all true simultaneously (training: all always false). locationEnabled=false (training: always true). isActive=false on infected machine - contradictory pattern never seen.

use_cases.name: UC-04: Supply Chain Compromise - Stale Agent with Active Threats
use_cases.description: Outdated agent version on a server with active threats and pending uninstall - classic supply chain compromise indicator. Old agent can't mitigate.

expected_triggers.autoencoder: 25 active threats (max reconstruction error on this feature) + quarantine + infected + scan aborted - extreme multi-feature deviation

use_cases.name: UC-05: Solarwinds Node Down with Anomalous Status
use_cases.description: Solarwinds node reporting Status=9 (critical/unreachable) with extreme NodeID - indicates infrastructure monitoring failure or device spoofing.

expected_triggers.autoencoder: Status=9 is rare extreme (training: 99.7% are Status=1). NodeID=99999 far outside training range (max=7340).

use_cases.name: UC-06: Mass Infection Outbreak - Server Farm
use_cases.description: Multiple indicators of a coordinated attack across a server cluster: infected, quarantined, firewall toggled, extreme threat count with max resources consumed.

expected_triggers.statistical: activeThreats z=50, totalMemory z=35.6, cpuCount z=29.2, coreCount z=51.9 - all extreme
expected_triggers.autoencoder: Maximum deviation across ALL feature dimensions simultaneously. Reconstruction error will be orders of magnitude higher than any normal record.

use_cases.name: UC-07: Lateral Movement - Network Pivot Point
use_cases.description: Laptop with containerized workload (unusual for laptop), infected but still connected, with multiple threats. Suggests compromised developer machine being used for lateral movement.

expected_triggers.statistical: activeThreats z=4.0; hasContainerizedWorkload flipped (always false in training)
expected_triggers.autoencoder: laptop+containerizedWorkload+infected+slim_mode+connected is a never-seen pattern combination. Autoencoder can't reconstruct this contradictory device profile.

use_cases.name: UC-08: Solarwinds Infrastructure Anomaly Cluster
use_cases.description: Three Solarwinds nodes with impossible Status values and NodeIDs outside known range - potential SNMP spoofing or monitoring system compromise.

expected_triggers.autoencoder: NodeID values completely outside training range (max=7340). Status values 5/7/9 extremely rare. Combined with Unknown vendor - total pattern mismatch.
